# host_package_pacman (Resource)

Manages a single Pacman package and keeps its install reason marked as explicit.

With the default `ignore_version = true`, the resource manages package presence and install reason without querying Pacman's sync databases for candidate or upgrade versions. Set it to false only when Terraform should plan upgrades to the latest available version.

`install_reason` is both desired and observed state. The only configurable policy is `explicit`. If an external package operation changes an installed package to a dependency, refresh reports `dependency` and the next apply marks it explicit again.

Pacman resources in one provider process share cached snapshots of the local installed-package and explicit-package databases. Concurrent refreshes coalesce duplicate reads, and every successful or failed mutation invalidates the cache before a later status read.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_package_pacman" "git" {
  name = "git"
}

resource "host_package_pacman" "neovim" {
  name           = "neovim"
  install_reason = "explicit"
}

resource "host_package_pacman" "ripgrep" {
  name = "ripgrep"
}
```

## Import

Import an installed package by name:

```shell
terraform import host_package_pacman.git git
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Pacman package name.

### Optional

- `autoremove` (Boolean) Remove unused dependencies recursively with `pacman -Rns` when this package is removed. Defaults to false.
- `ignore_version` (Boolean) Ignore available version updates. When true, the resource manages package presence and explicit install reason without planning upgrades for new candidate versions.
- `install_reason` (String) Desired and observed Pacman install reason. The only supported desired value is `explicit`; refresh records `dependency` when external package operations change the installed package to a dependency, causing the next plan to restore `explicit`.
- `version` (String) Package version policy. Only `latest` is currently supported. Used for upgrade planning only when `ignore_version` is false.

### Read-Only

- `candidate_version` (String) Latest Pacman package version from sync databases. Null when `ignore_version` is true.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `name`.
- `installed_version` (String) Installed Pacman package version.
